sections.paragraph:
εὔχομαι impf. εὐχόμην Ac 27:29 (Tdf., s. Proleg. p. 121) and ηὐχόμην Ro 9:3; Ac 27:29; fut. εὔξομαι LXX; 1 aor. εὐξάμην (also ηὐ-LXX); pf. ptc. ηὐγμένος Num 6:20 (s. prec. entry; Hom.+).

① to speak to or to make requests of God, pray abs. (LXX; TestSol 26:8 A 5 p. 81, 33; Just., D. 90, 5; 103, 8) τῷ θεῷ (Hom.; Thu. 3, 58, 5; X., Cyr. 7, 1, 1; Plut., Pericl. 156 [8, 6]; Epict. 1, 29, 37; OGI 219, 21; POxy 1298, 4; PRyl 244, 3; Sir 38:9; ApcMos 5:31; Philo, Ebr. 125; Jos., Ant. 14, 22; 18, 211; Just., D. 1:4; Orig., C. Cels. 8, 37, 12 al.—On εὔχ. τ. θεοῖς s. GGhedini, Aegyptus 2, 1921, 191ff) Ac 26:29; IRo 1:1. πρὸς τ. θεόν to God (X., Mem. 1, 3, 2, Symp. 4, 55; 2 Macc 9:13; 15:27; TestSol 18:42; EpArist 305) 2 Cor 13:7. That which is requested of God is expressed either by the simple acc. pray for someth. (Pind. et al.; Jos., Ant. 12, 98) 2 Cor 13:9; by the acc. w. inf. (Hom. et al.; TestSol 18:42; Did., Gen 73, 10) Ac 26:29; 2 Cor 13:7; or by prepositional phrases: εὐ. περί τινος GJs 8:3; 13:1; εὐ. περί τινος πρὸς τὸν κύριον Hs 5, 3, 7; εὐ. ὑπέρ τινος for someone (X., Mem. 2, 2, 10; Aeschin. 3:18 ὑπέρ τινος πρὸς τοὺς θεούς; Longus 2, 24, 2; Diog. L. 8, 9 εὔ. ὑπὲρ ἑαυτῶν; PMeyer 24, 4; TestGad 7:1; TestJos 18:2; ParJer 2:3; ApcMos 35; Jos., Ant. 11, 119; Just., D. 96, 3 al.) w. ὅπως foll. (ParJer 2:3 [cod. C]; Epict. 2, 16, 13; Polyaenus 1, 26 εὐ. Διὶ ὅπως; PRyl 244, 3 εὔχομαι θεοῖς πᾶσιν ὅπως ὑγιαίνοντας ὑμᾶς ἀπολάβω; Jos., Ant. 11, 17) Js 5:16; also ἵνα (cp. Dionys. Hal. 9, 53; Epict. 2, 6, 12; EpArist 45; Just., D. 35, 8 al.) Hs 5, 2, 10.—For lit. s. προσεύχομαι, end.

② wish (Pind. et al.; ins, pap, Philo; perh. Jos., Ant. 12, 98; cp. Mel., HE 4, 26, 8 εὐχή=wish) τὶ for someth. (X., Hell. 5, 1, 3) IMg 1:2. W. inf. foll. (Περὶ ὕψους 9, 10; Alexandr. graffito in UvWilamowitz, SBBerlAk 1902, 1098 εὔχομαι κἀγὼ ἐν τάχυ σὺν σοὶ εἶναι; SibOr 4, 111; Just., A I, 15, 6) ITr 10:1. Foll. by acc. and inf. (X., An. 1, 4, 7; Ep. 46 of Apoll. of Ty.: Philostrat. I 355, 24 K.; Just., D. 79, 2 al.) Ac 27:29; 3J 2 (cp. POxy 292, 11 [perh. 25 a.d.] πρὸ δὲ πάντων ὑγιαίνειν σε εὔχομαι; PFay 117, 27; PMich 203, 2); IEph 1:3; 2:1; ITr 12:3; IRo 5:2; ISm 11:1; 13:2; IPol 8:3; MPol 22:1. Foll. by nom. and inf. Ro 9:3, on the analogy of θέλω w. inf. (cp. Aeschyl., Eum. 429; TestReub 1:7)=opt. w. ἄν. Foll. by ἵνα μή IPhld 6:3. As a greeting formula εὐ. πλεῖστα χαίρειν IMg ins; ITr ins.—AAdkins, ClQ 19, ’69, 20–33 (Hom.).—M-M. EDNT. TW. Spicq. Sv.

impf. impf. = imperfect

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

prec. prec. = preceding

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

abs. abs. = absolute

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

acc. acc. = accusative

someth. someth. = something

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

inf. inf. = infinitive

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Aeschin Aeschin , IV b.c.; spurious letters II a.d.—List 5

Longus Longus , II ?a.d.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

PMeyer PMeyer = Papyrus ed. MM.: Griechische Texte aus Ägypten—List 4

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

foll. foll. = followed, following

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

perh. perh. = perhaps

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

PMich PMich = Michigan Papyri—List 4

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

TestReub TestReub = Testament of Reuben, s. Test12Patr—List 2

opt. opt. = optative

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

ClQ ClQ = Classical Quarterly—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 417
